user.title: Installation costs
user.description: Enter the financial details of your PV system. These values are the basis for all yield and amortization calculations.

data_description.installation_cost: Total cost of the PV system including hardware, installation, and any other one-time expenses. Used to calculate when the system breaks even.
data_description.commissioning_date: The date your PV system was first switched on. Used to determine system age — the integration needs at least a minimum number of days of history before projecting a break-even date.
data_description.historical_offset: Total money already saved or earned by the system before this integration was set up (e.g. if you track this in a spreadsheet). Leave at 0 if you are setting this up from day one.

entities.title: Energy sensors & pricing
entities.description: Select the sensors that provide your production and grid data, and choose how electricity prices and feed-in tariffs are sourced.

data_description.pv_production_entity: Sensor measuring the total energy your PV system has ever produced (kWh, state class: total_increasing). This is usually provided by your inverter integration.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor measuring the total energy exported to the grid (kWh, total_increasing). Self-consumption is calculated as production minus export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Optional. Sensor measuring total energy drawn from the grid (kWh, total_increasing). Only required for the self-sufficiency sensor.
data_description.feed_in_tariff_mode: Fixed: you enter a static rate in ct/kWh on the next screen. Entity: you select a sensor whose current value is used as the tariff (useful for variable tariffs).
data_description.electricity_price_mode: Fixed: you enter a static price in ct/kWh on the next screen. Entity: you select a sensor whose value is used as the price (e.g. Tibber, Awattar).
data_description.min_history_days: Minimum number of days since commissioning before a break-even date is projected. Prevents unreliable estimates when the system is brand new. Default: 60.
data_description.rolling_window_days: How many recent days are used to calculate the average daily yield for projecting the break-even date. A longer window gives a smoother estimate. Default: 365.

feed_in_tariff.title: Feed-in tariff
feed_in_tariff.description: Configure the rate at which exported energy is compensated.

data_description.feed_in_tariff_value: Your feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. Check your energy contract or grid operator confirmation.
data_description.feed_in_tariff_entity: Sensor providing the current feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. For accurate per-hour calculations, this sensor should have long-term statistics enabled.

electricity_price.title: Electricity price
electricity_price.description: Configure the price of electricity from the grid. This is used to calculate how much money you save by consuming your own solar power. {statistics_note}

data_description.electricity_price_value: Your electricity price in ct/kWh. Use the rate you pay for grid electricity, excluding any fixed fees.
data_description.electricity_price_entity: Sensor providing the current electricity price in ct/kWh. For accurate per-hour calculations, this sensor should have long-term statistics enabled.

init.title: Installation costs
init.description: Edit the financial details of your PV system.

data_description.installation_cost: Total cost of the PV system including hardware, installation, and any other one-time expenses.
data_description.commissioning_date: The date your PV system was first switched on.
data_description.historical_offset: Total money already saved or earned before this integration was set up. Leave at 0 if not applicable.

entities.title: Energy sensors & pricing
entities.description: Edit sensors, pricing modes, and update settings.

data_description.pv_production_entity: Sensor measuring total energy produced by your PV system (kWh, total_increasing).
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor measuring total energy exported to the grid (kWh, total_increasing).
data_description.grid_import_entity: Optional. Required for the self-sufficiency sensor.
data_description.feed_in_tariff_mode: Fixed: static rate in ct/kWh. Entity: sensor-based tariff.
data_description.electricity_price_mode: Fixed: static price in ct/kWh. Entity: sensor-based price.
data_description.min_history_days: Minimum days since commissioning before a break-even date is projected. Default: 60.
data_description.rolling_window_days: Recent days used for the break-even projection. Default: 365.
data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often the integration recalculates all values. Lower values increase recorder load.

feed_in_tariff.title: Feed-in tariff
feed_in_tariff.description: Edit the rate at which exported energy is compensated.

data_description.feed_in_tariff_entity: Sensor providing the current feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. Should have long-term statistics enabled.

electricity_price.title: Electricity price
electricity_price.description: Edit the price of electricity from the grid. {statistics_note}

data_description.electricity_price_entity: Sensor providing the current electricity price in ct/kWh. Should have long-term statistics enabled.
